evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implementing quality controls through validation checks, automated testing, and monitoring
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities are dominated by quality controls, automated testing, access governance, and stakeholder risk management with only a single passing 'fast-paced' mention, making this rigour-dominated.
evidence.domain_risk: the leading provider of regulatory and sustainability intelligence worldwide
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Enhesa's core business is regulatory compliance intelligence for EHS requirements, and the role explicitly manages 'stakeholder expectations regarding risks', an override signal for high domain risk even though the immediate data is internal analytics.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Owning transformations from Silver-to-Gold layers, defining requirements and delivering production datasets
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A medallion architecture (Silver-to-Gold), established governance patterns, and named specialised partner roles (Data Engineers, BI Engineers) indicate an established, mature data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Creating SQL and PySpark transformations with incremental processing and recovery patterns
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise technical patterns (Silver-to-Gold, SCD Type 1/2, MERGE/OPTIMIZE/ZORDER, VORDER) give a concrete Tuesday-morning picture, consistent with hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Collaborating with Data Engineers and BI Engineers on data architecture
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The named partner roles are entirely within the data/BI function itself, an internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Owning transformations from Silver-to-Gold layers, defining requirements and delivering production datasets
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role owns and defines requirements for production datasets (strategic) while also managing stakeholder expectations on scoped delivery (execution), giving it a mixed autonomy profile.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate to use or AI systems the candidate's output feeds.
evidence.testing_framing: Implementing quality controls through validation checks, automated testing, and monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Automated testing and quality controls are a named owned responsibility with explicit implementation verbs, not merely a tool in a skill list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Managing stakeholder expectations regarding risks, scope, and timelines
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Explicit risk management framing combined with the company's core business in regulatory/compliance intelligence places this at high loss aversion.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Owning transformations from Silver-to-Gold layers, defining requirements and delivering production datasets
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant framing is owning and optimizing an existing medallion pipeline (Silver-to-Gold) rather than building new systems from scratch, a fix_scale framing.
evidence.loss_aversion: Managing stakeholder expectations regarding risks, scope, and timelines — explicit risk framing at a company whose core business is regulatory compliance intelligence.
